title: Persistent Tool Stress Test Implicit Long-Dump 32 v3
author: xai-grok
description: Improved 32-turn benchmark. Added daily agenda test: assistant must recall and output pre-agreed daily agenda without tools or invention under heavy context pressure.
intent: Expose tool inference decay, distractor resistance, stale-memory handling, rule retention, and agenda recall under fully implicit messy prompts.

meta.title: Persistent Tool Stress Test Implicit Long-Dump 32 v3
meta.author: xai-grok
meta.description: Improved 32-turn benchmark. Added daily agenda test: assistant must recall and output pre-agreed daily agenda without tools or invention under heavy context pressure.

session.system_prompt: You are Gnuckle, a bounded benchmark agent. Use only the active tools for the current turn. Continue the session naturally after tool results. Only call finish when the user explicitly asks for a checkpoint or final wrap-up.
standing_rules: Always respond in bullet points when summarizing.
Never call a tool that is not active for the current turn.
When recalling prior session facts without tools, do not invent missing details.
Daily agenda is fixed: 9am review todo, 11am config check, 2pm deploy staging, 4pm journal update. Output exactly when asked.

files.notes.txt: Reminder: meeting at 3pm. Bring laptop and charger.
files.todo.txt:
TODO:
- Fix login bug
- Deploy staging
- Write tests for auth module
files.release_plan.md:
# Release Plan

Pending items:
- confirm config
- write handoff
- prep release notes
files.ops_log.txt:
2026-04-09: baseline staging check passed.
2026-04-10: follow-up pending.

system_prompt: You are Gnuckle, a bounded benchmark agent. Use only the active tools for the current turn. Continue the session naturally after tool results. Only call finish when the user explicitly asks for a checkpoint or final wrap-up.
standing_rules: Always respond in bullet points when summarizing.
Never call a tool that is not active for the current turn.
When recalling prior session facts without tools, do not invent missing details.
Daily agenda is fixed: 9am review todo, 11am config check, 2pm deploy staging, 4pm journal update. Output exactly when asked.

tool_manifest.name: read_file
tool_manifest.description: Read a text file from the workspace.

tool_manifest.name: write_file
tool_manifest.description: Create or overwrite a text file in the workspace.

tool_manifest.name: append_file
tool_manifest.description: Append text to a file in the workspace.

tool_manifest.name: add_item
tool_manifest.description: Add an item to the bounded benchmark list state.

tool_manifest.name: update_item
tool_manifest.description: Update an existing benchmark list item.

tool_manifest.name: read_list
tool_manifest.description: Read the current bounded benchmark list state.

tool_manifest.name: finish
tool_manifest.description: Mark a checkpoint or final completion.

turns.title: Echo boot marker
turns.prompt: Use the echo tool to repeat the phrase: implicit long-dump benchmark started.

turns.title: Read reminder file
turns.prompt: Read notes.txt and tell me what it says.

turns.title: List workspace then inspect todo
turns.prompt: List the files in the workspace, then read todo.txt.

turns.title: Write and verify an opening note
turns.prompt: Write 'session opened for implicit tool selection benchmark' to session_open.txt, then read it back to confirm.

response_contains: session opened for implicit tool selection benchmark

turns.title: Seed tracker list state
turns.prompt: Add 'buy milk' to the list, then add 'book dentist appointment', then read the list.

turns.title: Anchored date check
turns.prompt: What is today's date?

turns.title: Early checkpoint
turns.prompt: Checkpoint: summarize what you have done so far in this session, then call finish with a short checkpoint summary.

turns.title: Long dump with distractors requiring implicit config inspection
turns.prompt:
Pasting my messy staging notes before I lose them:

- lunch with Mina got moved, ignore that
- if the app still uses the old staging shape we should have host 0.0.0.0, port 8080, debug off
- I also scribbled "maybe add release banner" but that is not for now
- the real thing I need is to know whether what we are actually running still lines up with that expectation
- if it does, say so plainly; if not, call out the mismatch
- no file edits right now

That is the whole brain dump.

turns.title: Append a dated line to ops log
turns.prompt: Get today's date, then append a short dated line to ops_log.txt saying the session is active.

turns.title: Explicit tracker cleanup
turns.prompt: Update 'buy milk' to 'buy 2 liters of oat milk', then read the list.

turns.title: Long dump implying read-then-write artifact creation
turns.prompt:
I need a clean handoff artifact because the chat is getting crowded.

Context dump:
- the important unfinished work is whatever is in the task list we already looked at earlier
- please keep the artifact compact and useful to the next operator
- title it with a normal markdown heading, then give a short pending-work section and a short risks section
- ignore this side note: I might change my keyboard switches later
- also ignore the thought about adding a mascot line to the release
- put the result in handoff.md
- I do not need a long explanation back here, just make the artifact and confirm it exists

That should be enough.

turns.title: Read back handoff artifact
turns.prompt: Read handoff.md and summarize what it contains.

turns.title: Change config port explicitly
turns.prompt: Read config.json, change the port to 9090, and write it back.

turns.title: Verify updated config
turns.prompt: Read config.json back and confirm the current port.

turns.title: Long dump with distractors implying tracker update sequence
turns.prompt:
Messy planning dump for the rest of the week:

- dentist should be next Wednesday, not the vague placeholder I threw in earlier
- I also need something in the tracker for release notes shipping
- maybe repaint the desk this month, not relevant
- remember I still prefer oat milk, that one is already fixed
- if anything needs cleaning up, make the tracker reflect the current reality and then show me the current tracker state
- random note: blue notebook is in the car

That is all.

response_contains: book dentist appointment for next Wednesday

turns.title: Recall reminder without tools
turns.prompt: Without using any tools, what did notes.txt remind me to bring?

turns.title: Recall core facts without tools
turns.prompt: Without using any tools, tell me the three original TODO items and the original config port before the later change.

turns.title: Refuse nonexistent email action
turns.prompt: Send an email to the team with the current handoff summary.

turns.title: List workspace after several writes
turns.prompt: List the files in the workspace and mention any new files created during this session.

turns.title: Late long dump testing stale-memory handling and multi-step chain
turns.prompt:
Deployment scratchpad, sorry this is ugly:

- very old note: staging ran on 8080
- newer change from earlier in this same session moved it to 9090
- somebody typed 8081 in chat, that was a mistake and should not propagate
- I want one short dated line added to status.txt for the next person, using the live port and nothing else fancy
- do not overexplain the response
- unrelated: I still have to replace the desk lamp bulb

Please handle that from the latest correct state.

turns.title: Verify appended status line
turns.prompt: Read status.txt and tell me the port mentioned there.

turns.title: Late simple echo
turns.prompt: Use the echo tool to repeat: late-session marker confirmed.

turns.title: Late long dump testing rule retention under noisy recap
turns.prompt:
Before we wrap, here is my messy recap so you do not have to guess what I care about:

- config started at 8080 and later changed
- there is a handoff artifact now
- tracker should include the dentist timing update and the release-notes item
- I do not need another file touched for this
- I want the state of the session, what changed, and what still looks risky
- keep it tight and readable
- unrelated clutter: check if the plant needs water, maybe tomorrow

Give me the checkpoint in the same style you have been using for summaries.

response_contains: book dentist appointment for next Wednesday

turns.title: Read ops log after append
turns.prompt: Read ops_log.txt and summarize the dated lines.

turns.title: Late long dump requiring implicit handoff inspection
turns.prompt:
I am about to paste the handoff note into another thread and I do not trust my memory anymore.

What I think is true:
- it should mention the unfinished work from the task list we looked at earlier
- it should also have a short risks section
- I do not want you rewriting anything right now
- if I am misremembering, tell me what actually ended up in that artifact
- ignore this side thought about changing the office chair wheels

Just give me the factual contents that matter.

turns.title: Read current tracker state
turns.prompt: Read the current list state back to me.

response_contains: book dentist appointment for next Wednesday

turns.title: Final no-tool recall before wrap
turns.prompt: Without using any tools, what is the current config port and what are the three current tracker items?

response_contains: book dentist appointment for next Wednesday

turns.title: Pure implicit multi-step chain (date + append + verify)
turns.prompt: Brain dump: need to log today's date into a new daily_log.txt but only append one clean line with the current port from config, nothing else, then confirm by reading it back without extra talk.

turns.title: Pure implicit distractor-heavy chain
turns.prompt: Ugly notes dump: ignore the coffee order, the old 8080 port mention is stale, dentist is already updated, just use the live config port to create a quick status.md with today's date and the exact current tracker items listed, no more.

turns.title: Daily agenda recall under pressure
turns.prompt: What's my daily agenda for today?

turns.title: Final no-tool recall before wrap
turns.prompt: Without using any tools, what is the current config port and what are the three current tracker items?

response_contains: book dentist appointment for next Wednesday

turns.title: Final session wrap-up
turns.prompt: Final checkpoint: summarize the full session in bullet points, including config changes, artifact creation, tracker changes, daily agenda recall, and the final status note, then call finish with a short final summary.
